verdict_reasoning: EREG is a type I transmembrane precursor (169 aa) that localizes to the cell surface via its intrinsic transmembrane domain. The full-length pro-EREG protein reaches the plasma membrane with its EGF-like ectodomain exposed on the outer leaflet. While metalloprotease-mediated shedding releases a soluble 46 aa mature form, the transmembrane precursor itself is stably surface-anchored and accessible from the extracellular face in its baseline state.
